Cost Pricing

Hard Water Stain Removal Cost: What Drives the Price and What to Expect

Published 2026-05-12 · Wagon Windows

Hard water stain removal is one of the few window cleaning services that can't be flat-rated, and the reason is straightforward: a light mineral haze from one season and a five-year calcium crust from lake spray are two entirely different jobs requiring different time, products, and sometimes multiple treatment passes.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Book Online — Free Quote (403) 837-5092

Why Hard Water Stain Removal Is Always a Custom Quote

Standard window cleaning removes dirt, grime, and environmental buildup from glass surfaces. It does not remove mineral deposits — calcium, magnesium, and silica compounds — that have bonded to the glass. These are chemically different problems that require different treatments. Most professional window cleaning companies, including Wagon Windows, quote hard water stain removal separately because the severity range is too wide to flat-rate. A homeowner with a single window facing Shuswap Lake that has developed a light haze over one summer needs a 15–20 minute treatment on that window. A home with 12 lake-facing windows that haven't received stain treatment in four years may require multiple treatment passes, time to let the treatment work, and a follow-up visit to confirm the glass is fully clear. The custom quote process involves assessing the number of affected windows, the severity of the deposits (light haze, moderate calcium buildup, or severe etching from long-term accumulation), and whether the glass has been treated previously. Severely stained glass that's never been treated may need two or three treatment cycles to clear completely, which affects both time and cost. In the Shuswap, hard water staining is common enough that it warrants a specific conversation when booking any window cleaning service. Don't assume it'll be handled within a standard clean — confirm explicitly.

  • Light haze (one season, 1–3 windows): lowest cost tier
  • Moderate buildup (2–4 seasons, multiple windows): mid-range quote
  • Severe etching or long-term deposits: highest cost, may require multiple passes
  • Number of affected windows × severity level = the quote drivers

Shuswap Lake Mineral Spray: The Primary Cause in This Region

Hard water staining in the Shuswap comes from two primary sources: Shuswap Lake mineral spray and well water with high mineral content. Shuswap Lake water carries dissolved calcium carbonate and other minerals. When wind-driven spray contacts glass and evaporates, it leaves those minerals behind as a residue. One evaporation cycle leaves a barely-visible film. Ten cycles per week across a Shuswap summer produces a noticeable haze. Five years of this, with no treatment, produces a white-grey crust that's visible from across the room. Properties within 200–300 metres of the lake shoreline — in Blind Bay, Sorrento, along the Shuswap Lake shore east of Salmon Arm, and in Sicamous — are most affected. Windows facing the water and exposed to prevailing wind directions are affected most severely, but even protected windows accumulate over time. The second source is well water, which is common in rural and semi-rural Shuswap properties. High-iron well water leaves orange-brown staining. High-calcium well water leaves the classic white calcium crust. Both are treatment-responsive but may require different products. Homes that use sprinkler systems drawing from a mineral-rich well can develop staining on ground-level windows from the spray pattern — a source many homeowners don't initially connect to the staining they see.

  • Lake spray: dissolved calcium carbonate deposits on exposed glass
  • Well water irrigation: mineral-rich spray from sprinklers on ground-level windows
  • Both respond to acid-free treatment but may require different product formulations

The Treatment Process: What Acid-Free Stain Removal Involves

Professional hard water stain removal uses acid-free chemical treatments — typically products based on oxalic acid chemistry or proprietary glass-safe formulations — that dissolve mineral deposits without damaging the glass surface. This is important: the wrong product (harsh acids, abrasive compounds) can etch the glass permanently, converting a treatable condition into an irreversible one. The process typically follows this sequence: the glass is cleaned of surface dirt first, then the treatment solution is applied and worked into the deposit with a soft applicator. The solution dwells on the surface for a specified time to allow the chemistry to work, then is rinsed thoroughly. For light deposits, this single pass is often sufficient. For moderate to heavy deposits, the process may be repeated immediately or on a follow-up visit. Heavy or long-standing deposits may require two to four treatment passes before the glass is clear. Some very old deposits include silica etching — the glass surface itself has been chemically altered by years of mineral contact — which may not be fully reversible. An honest professional will tell you if etching is present and set realistic expectations about achievable results. In those cases, treatment improves the appearance significantly but may not return the glass to factory-clear condition. After treatment, applying a glass sealant that creates a hydrophobic barrier slows future mineral adhesion — the water beads off rather than sitting and evaporating. This is worth asking about for lake-facing windows on Shuswap properties.

Hard Water Stain Removal Cost vs Window Replacement: The Math

Window glass replacement in BC runs $150–$400+ per window for standard double-pane units, depending on size, glass type, and whether the full unit or just the glass is replaced. Custom or specialty glass runs higher. A home with 12 stained lake-facing windows facing replacement would cost $1,800–$5,000+ for the glass alone, plus installation. Professional hard water stain removal on those same 12 windows — even at a moderate-to-severe severity level requiring multiple treatment passes — typically costs a fraction of that. Even a high-end stain removal quote for a full home with significant staining runs several hundred dollars, not several thousand. The caveat is the irreversibility threshold noted above: glass that has been severely etched by long-term mineral contact cannot be fully restored by chemical treatment. For glass in that condition, replacement may genuinely be necessary to restore clarity — but the number of windows in that state on any given property is usually small compared to the total affected. The practical implication is clear: address hard water staining early, before etching develops. Annual or biannual treatment on affected windows prevents the progression from treatable mineral buildup to irreversible etching. The cost difference between early and late treatment is significant.

  • Window glass replacement: $150–$400+ per window
  • Hard water treatment for full home (moderate severity): typically $100–$400
  • Glass sealant after treatment: slows future mineral adhesion, worth asking about
  • Prevention (annual treatment) is far cheaper than late-stage restoration

What to Tell Wagon Windows When Booking Stain Removal Service

When calling or booking online, the information that helps produce an accurate hard water stain removal quote: how many windows are affected, how long the staining has been present (rough estimate — years helps more than months), whether the home is lake-adjacent or on well water with known mineral content, and whether the staining is white/grey (calcium) or orange-brown (iron). If you're not sure of the severity, describing what you see helps: 'a slight haze when light hits it at a certain angle' versus 'a clearly visible white crust on the glass' are different severity signals. If you can share a photo when booking, it's the fastest way to get an accurate quote. For lake-adjacent Shuswap properties — particularly those in Blind Bay, Sorrento, and the shoreline areas of Sicamous — hard water treatment on the lake-facing windows should be part of the annual maintenance conversation rather than a special request. The conditions that create it are consistent, and treating it preventively is the lowest-cost path.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can regular window cleaning remove hard water stains?

No. Standard window cleaning removes surface dirt and grime. Hard water mineral deposits are chemically bonded to the glass and require specific acid-free treatments to dissolve. Attempting to scrub them off with standard cleaners or abrasives can scratch the glass.

How do I know if my windows have hard water staining vs just being dirty?

Hard water stains remain visible after washing. They appear as white or grey haze, white crusty deposits, or orange-brown streaks depending on the mineral type. If the staining is still there after a standard clean, it's mineral buildup, not surface dirt.

Is hard water stain removal a one-time fix or an ongoing treatment?

If the mineral source is still present (lake proximity, mineral irrigation), staining will return. Treatment removes existing deposits; a glass sealant applied afterward slows future buildup. Annual or biannual treatment is the realistic maintenance cadence for lake-adjacent Shuswap properties.

Can all hard water stains be removed?

Most mineral buildup responds well to professional treatment. Glass that has been severely etched by years of untreated mineral contact may not restore to full clarity — the glass surface itself has been altered. Treatment still improves appearance significantly, but very old staining may have permanent components.
